title: Twilight of the Idols
description: Twilight of the Idols — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "偶像的黄昏" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Complete table of contents for Twilight of the Idols and supplementary works'

relations.label: Chapter 2: Morality as the Enemy of Nature
Chapter 6: Skirmishes in a War with the Age
Chapter 7: Things I Owe to the Ancients

memes.label: Amor Fati
memes.description: A key philosophical concept in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Anti Nature
memes.description: A concept from Nietzsche's philosophical vocabulary in Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Apollonian
memes.description: The aesthetic principle of dream-vision, individuation, and measured form — counterpart to the Dionysian in Nietzsche's analysis of Greek tragedy

memes.label: Appearance
memes.description: Nietzsche's concept of appearance as the only accessible reality, reversing the Platonic devaluation of the apparent world in favor of the "true world."

memes.label: Aristotle
memes.description: Ancient Greek philosopher whose theory of tragic catharsis Nietzsche critiqued and whose diagnosis of pity as a morbid state Nietzsche deployed polemically against Schopenhauer.

memes.label: Ascetic Ideal
memes.description: The life-denying moral framework that Nietzsche identifies as the engine of Western civilization — the priestly valuation that treats instinct, sensuality, and life itself as guilty

memes.label: Athens
memes.description: Ancient Greek city-state central to Nietzsche's discussion of Greek philosophy

memes.label: Bayreuth
memes.description: A place referenced in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Beauty
memes.description: Nietzsche's naturalistic account of beauty as a human projection grounded in sexual instinct and physiological affirmation, not a property of objects or a Platonic Form.

memes.label: Otto von Bismarck
memes.description: The Prussian statesman and architect of German unification whom Nietzsche invokes as a symbol of strategic ruthlessness — the "forger of the Ems dispatch" and practitioner of Realpolitik

memes.label: Buddha (Siddhartha Gautama)
memes.description: The founder of Buddhism, whose teaching Nietzsche contrasts favourably with Christianity as a "hygienic" religion — one that combats depression not through faith but through physiological regimen, representing a noble pessimism rather than a morality of *ressentiment*.

memes.label: Buddhism
memes.description: Nietzsche's comparative case study of a "religion of decadence" that, unlike Christianity, is honest about its nihilism and offers a realistic path to the cessation of suffering

memes.label: Thomas Carlyle
memes.description: Scottish historian and essayist whose "heroico-moral interpretation of dyspeptic moods" Nietzsche diagnosed as disguised pessimism and intellectual dishonesty.

memes.label: Causality
memes.description: The cognitive instinct Nietzsche identifies as the source of one of the "Four Great Errors" — the tendency to project inner experiences of willing onto the external world as though they revealed causal structure

memes.label: Cesare Borgia
memes.description: Italian Renaissance cardinal, condottiero, and political figure whom Nietzsche celebrated as an embodiment of vital, amoral strength — the "higher man" who stands as the antithesis of Christian moral timidity.

memes.label: "Chapter 2: Morality as the Enemy of Nature"
"Chapter 6: Skirmishes in a War with the Age"
"Chapter 7: Things I Owe to the Ancients"
Auguste Comte
memes.description: French philosopher and founder of positivism and sociology, whom Nietzsche dismissed as a Jesuitical figure attempting to restore Catholic structures through the "circuitous route of science."
aliases: Isidore Auguste Marie François Xavier Comte

memes.label: Confucius
memes.description: The Chinese sage whose ethical teaching Nietzsche places alongside Manu and Plato in the company of those "improvers" of mankind who never doubted their right to the pious fraud — the lawgiver as moral artist whose greatness lies in the power of his legislation, not its truth.

memes.label: Conscience
memes.description: The inner tribunal of moral judgment that Nietzsche genealogically traces to the internalization of cruelty — "bad conscience" is the violence of the instinct for freedom turned inward against the self

memes.label: Dante Alighieri
memes.description: Italian poet of the Divine Comedy, whom Nietzsche savagely characterized in the "My Impossible People" section as "the hyena that writes poetry in tombs."

memes.label: Charles Darwin
memes.description: The English naturalist whose theory of evolution Nietzsche critiques in "Anti-Darwin" — arguing that the struggle for existence produces mediocrity, not excellence

memes.label: David Strauss
memes.description: German theologian and "free-spirit" whose landmark *Life of Jesus* demythologised the Gospels — and whom Nietzsche, after an early infatuation, came to dismiss as a cautionary example of German cultural philistinism masquerading as enlightenment.

memes.label: Death of God
memes.description: A key philosophical concept in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Death Of God Event
memes.description: A historical or cultural event referenced in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Death of Tragedy
memes.description: The historical demise of Greek tragedy as an art form, which Nietzsche attributes to the rationalistic influence of Euripides and Socrates — a pivotal event in his narrative of cultural decline

memes.label: Decadence
memes.description: Nietzsche's concept of décadence as the degeneration of life instincts and the triumph of reactive values over active ones

memes.label: Decline Of German Culture
memes.description: A historical or cultural event referenced in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: René Descartes
memes.description: French philosopher and mathematician whose mechanistic view of animals and mind-body dualism Nietzsche both admired and radically rejected.

memes.label: Dionysian
memes.description: A key philosophical concept in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Fyodor Dostoevsky
memes.description: Russian novelist and profound psychologist, whom Nietzsche called "the only psychologist from whom I had anything to learn"

memes.label: Egypt
memes.description: Ancient Egypt, which Nietzsche invokes through the metaphor of "Egyptianism" — the philosophical tendency to deny Becoming and seek eternal Being

memes.label: Ralph Waldo Emerson
memes.description: American essayist and transcendentalist philosopher whom Nietzsche admired for his cheerful wisdom and lightness of spirit, contrasting him favourably with Thomas Carlyle's ponderous seriousness.

memes.label: Epicurus
memes.description: Ancient Greek materialist philosopher whom Nietzsche praised as an "atypical decadent" and the great opponent of subterranean cults — a genuine deliverance crushed by St. Paul.

memes.label: Error
memes.description: Nietzsche's concept of error as the fundamental mode of human cognition, encompassing the Four Great Errors and the "error of Being."

memes.label: Eternal Recurrence
memes.description: Nietzsche's thought-experiment that all events recur infinitely — the ultimate test of life-affirmation

memes.label: Europe
memes.description: European civilization as the primary object of Nietzsche's cultural diagnosis — a continent in decline, losing the instincts that once produced great institutions

memes.label: France
memes.description: The French cultural sphere Nietzsche engages as both a source of psychological sophistication and a case study in modern decline — from classical grandeur to revolutionary leveling

memes.label: Free Will
memes.description: The concept Nietzsche systematically dismantles in Twilight of the Idols as a theological error designed to make mankind "guilty" and therefore controllable

memes.label: French Revolution
memes.description: A historical or cultural event referenced in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Genealogy
memes.description: A concept from Nietzsche's philosophical vocabulary in Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Genius
memes.description: Nietzsche's critique of the "genius" as a theological category projected onto exceptional individuals, obscuring the accumulated labour, discipline, and historical conditions that produce greatness.

memes.label: George Sand
memes.description: French Romantic novelist whose prose style Nietzsche subjects to a withering critique — the "lactea ubertas," an insufferably self-complacent, false, and cold writer whom he associates with Rousseau's corrupting influence on French taste.

memes.label: The German Empire
memes.description: The founding of the German Empire in 1871, which Nietzsche diagnoses as a symptom and accelerant of German cultural decline — political power purchased at the cost of intellectual greatness

memes.label: Germany
memes.description: A place referenced in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
memes.description: A philosopher discussed in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Goncourt Brothers
memes.description: French literary brothers whom Nietzsche condemns as "the greatest sinners against style" and the supreme practitioners of note-book psychology — a method of literary observation that substitutes restless accumulation of trivial detail for genuine artistic vision.

memes.label: Ancient Greece
memes.description: The ancient Greek world — for Nietzsche the supreme model of cultural health and the site of both the highest achievement (tragedy) and the most consequential decline (the Socratic turn)

memes.label: G. W. F. Hegel
memes.description: German idealist philosopher whose system Nietzsche dismisses as a "European event" of dubious value — the grand systematiser of the metaphysical tradition that Nietzsche's philosophy seeks to dismantle.

memes.label: Heinrich Heine
memes.description: German poet and literary critic whom Nietzsche ranked alongside Goethe, Hegel, and Schopenhauer as a European-level intellectual event.

memes.label: Heraclitus
memes.description: A figure discussed in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Homer
memes.description: Legendary Greek epic poet of the Iliad and Odyssey, referenced by Nietzsche in the "My Impossible People" section in connection with the Goncourt brothers as "the two Ajaxes fighting with Homer."

memes.label: Idol
memes.description: A concept from Nietzsche's philosophical vocabulary in Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: India
memes.description: The ancient Indian civilization Nietzsche references as the source of both the Law-Book of Manu and Buddhism — a non-Western moral system he contrasts with Christianity

memes.label: Instinct
memes.description: The pre-rational biological and psychological drives that Nietzsche regards as the authentic foundation of all valuation

memes.label: Jacob Burckhardt
memes.description: Swiss historian of art and culture, Nietzsche's colleague and friend at the University of Basel, whom Nietzsche regarded as a rare exemplar of genuine culture and the most profound modern connoisseur of Greek civilisation.

memes.label: Jerusalem
memes.description: A place referenced in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Jesus of Nazareth
memes.description: The founder of Christianity, whom Nietzsche reinterpreted in The Antichrist as a "free spirit" and psychological type fundamentally at odds with the institutional Church that claimed his name

memes.label: Judaism
memes.description: The religious tradition Nietzsche identifies as the origin of the "slave revolt in morality" and the source of the concepts — sin, guilt, redemption — that Christianity universalized

memes.label: Immanuel Kant
memes.description: A philosopher discussed in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz
memes.description: German rationalist philosopher and mathematician whom Nietzsche groups with Luther and Kant as an intellectual "brake" on European thought — a figure of immense systematic ambition whose optimism Nietzsche cannot abide.

memes.label: Christian August Lobeck
memes.description: German classical philologist whose superficial treatment of the Dionysian mysteries was sharply criticized by Nietzsche

memes.label: Martin Luther
memes.description: The German monk and Reformation leader whom Nietzsche analyzes as both the restorer of Christianity and the archetype of the theologian whose moral fanaticism shaped German thought

memes.label: Manu
memes.description: The legendary Hindu lawgiver whose code Nietzsche analyses at length as the archetype of the "morality of breeding" — an Aryan legislation of order, hierarchy, and purity that stands as the polar opposite of Christianity's Chandala morality.

memes.label: Metaphysics
memes.description: Nietzsche's critique of metaphysics as a linguistic seduction that posits a "true world" behind appearances, culminating in Christianity as "the metaphysics of the hangman."

memes.label: John Stuart Mill
memes.description: British philosopher and political economist whose utilitarian clarity Nietzsche dismissed as "offensive lucidity" in the Skirmishes aphorisms.

memes.label: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
memes.description: The composer of *The Magic Flute*, whose libretto Nietzsche quotes in a satirical passage on literary self-consciousness — a fleeting but revealing appearance that connects Mozart's Enlightenment opera to Nietzsche's diagnosis of modern artistic pretension.

memes.label: Napoleon Bonaparte
memes.description: The French emperor whom Nietzsche celebrates as the supreme example of the "return to nature" and the embodiment of the great human being — a counter-ideal to the Christian "good man"

memes.label: Nihilism
memes.description: The radical devaluation of all values — the condition in which the highest values devalue themselves

memes.label: Jacques Offenbach
memes.description: German-born French composer of operettas whose music Nietzsche reduced to a one-word dismissal in the Skirmishes aphorisms.

memes.label: Blaise Pascal
memes.description: French mathematician and Jansenist thinker, regarded by Nietzsche as the exemplary case of Christian self-corruption.

memes.label: Paul the Apostle
memes.description: A figure discussed in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Pessimism
memes.description: Nietzsche's distinction between the "pessimism of weakness" (Schopenhauer's life-denial) and "Dionysian pessimism" (the tragic affirmation of life despite its terror and suffering).

memes.label: Saint Peter
memes.description: Apostle of Jesus and first Bishop of Rome, whom Nietzsche cited as a target of Christianity's offense against noble humanity through the doctrine of universal immortality.

memes.label: Pontius Pilate
memes.description: Roman governor of Judaea who presided over the trial of Jesus; the only figure in the New Testament whom Nietzsche regarded with unqualified respect.

memes.label: Plato
memes.description: A philosopher discussed in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Pre-Socratics
memes.description: The early Greek philosophers before Socrates whom Nietzsche regards as representing a healthier, pre-decadent mode of philosophical thinking — contrasted with the Socratic-Platonic degeneration

memes.label: Psychology
memes.description: Nietzsche's concept of psychology as the master science — a genealogical method for exposing the hidden physiological and power-driven roots of all philosophical and moral convictions.

memes.label: Raphael
memes.description: The Renaissance painter whom Nietzsche invokes in a single, decisive sentence to distinguish the affirmative artist from the life-denying Christian: "Raphael said Yea, Raphael *did* Yea — consequently Raphael was no Christian."

memes.label: Reality
memes.description: Nietzsche's critique of "reality" as a metaphysical fiction — the invented counter-concept to appearance that philosophy has historically privileged.

memes.label: Ernest Renan
memes.description: French historian and philologist whose intellectual dishonesty and priest-like seductiveness Nietzsche excoriated as symptomatic of modern decadence.

memes.label: Ressentiment
memes.description: A key philosophical concept in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Rome
memes.description: A place referenced in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
memes.description: The Genevan philosopher whom Nietzsche identifies as a key source of modern ressentiment, romanticism, and the "return to nature" — a figure he both analyzes and despises

memes.label: Russia
memes.description: The Russian Empire as Nietzsche's counterexample to Western European decline — "the only great nation to-day that has some lasting power and grit in her"

memes.label: Friedrich Schiller
memes.description: The German poet and dramatist whom Nietzsche critiques as the "moral trumpeter of Säckingen" — a figure whose popularity represents the German inability to recognize true greatness

memes.label: Arthur Schopenhauer
memes.description: A philosopher discussed in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: William Shakespeare
memes.description: English playwright and poet, referenced in Twilight of the Idols through a translator's adaptation of a line from A Midsummer Night's Dream.

memes.label: Slave Morality
memes.description: Nietzsche's term for the reactive moral system that inverts aristocratic values, originating in resentment (ressentiment)

memes.label: Socrates (Concept)
memes.description: A key philosophical concept in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Socrates
memes.description: A philosopher discussed in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: The Socratic Turn
memes.description: The pivotal shift in Greek culture when Socratic rationalism displaced tragic wisdom — Nietzsche's diagnosis of the "murder" of tragedy by dialectical optimism

memes.label: Soul
memes.description: Nietzsche's critique of the soul as an imaginary cause, a metaphysical fiction projected onto inner experience, and the "peace of the soul" as a misunderstanding of physiological states.

memes.label: Herbert Spencer
memes.description: English philosopher and sociologist who applied evolutionary theory to society, whom Nietzsche condemned as a décadent for celebrating altruism as the triumph of social evolution.

memes.label: Baruch Spinoza
memes.description: A figure discussed in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Stendhal
memes.description: French novelist and psychological realist whom Nietzsche ranked alongside Dostoevsky as one of the happiest discoveries of his intellectual life.

memes.label: The Church
memes.description: The institutional Christian Church as Nietzsche's primary case study of an organization built on the negation of life and the systematic "improvement" of mankind through weakening

memes.label: The Crucifixion
memes.description: A historical or cultural event referenced in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: The Four Great Errors Concept
memes.description: A concept from Nietzsche's philosophical vocabulary in Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: The Priesthood
memes.description: The priestly caste that Nietzsche identifies as the historical architects of moral valuation, ascetic discipline, and the "improvement" of mankind through weakening

memes.label: The Reformation
memes.description: A historical or cultural event referenced in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Thucydides
memes.description: Ancient Greek historian and the greatest representative of Sophistic realism, whom Nietzsche revered as the antidote to Platonism and the ideal of courageous, clear-eyed engagement with reality.

memes.label: Tragedy
memes.description: The art form central to Nietzsche's aesthetics — born from the synthesis of Apollonian and Dionysian impulses, and killed (according to Nietzsche) by Socratic rationalism

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: The epistemic ideal that Nietzsche subjects to genealogical critique — the "will to truth" is not a pure drive toward knowledge but a disguised moral impulse with ascetic origins

memes.label: Ubermensch
memes.description: A concept from Nietzsche's philosophical vocabulary in Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Ugly
memes.description: Nietzsche's concept of ugliness as a physiological-aesthetic judgment expressing the exhaustion or strengthening of the will to power.

memes.label: Victor Hugo
memes.description: The towering figure of French Romanticism whom Nietzsche dismisses in a single devastating aphorism as "the lighthouse on the sea of nonsense" — a master of grandiose rhetoric whose apparent warmth conceals the constitutive coldness of the Romantic artist.

memes.label: Richard Wagner
memes.description: A figure discussed in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Will to Power
memes.description: Nietzsche's fundamental principle that the basic drive of all life is not self-preservation but the expansion of power

memes.label: Zarathustra
memes.description: A figure discussed in Nietzsche's Twilight of the Idols

memes.label: Émile Zola
memes.description: French novelist and leading figure of literary naturalism whom Nietzsche dismissed in a single caustic aphorism as "the love of stinking."
